title: 1981-04-16_Pat_132.txt
key_claims: The "Two Source Cosmogony" in *VALIS* is actually a representation of an internal dialectic *within* the mind of God (as first envisioned by Jakob Böhme), not a battle between God and Satan.
The dark side of this dialectic is God's prerational/irrational Will; the bright side is God's reason (Logos/Hagia Sophia). The Logos side always wins the synthesis.
Dick's 2-74 meta-abstraction was his personal experience of this synthesis. It was Hegel's "Absolute Spirit" resolving the dialectic within Dick's mind, producing sanity (Logos).
In *BTA*, Angel Archer represents the Logos (Hagia Sophia) within Dick. Paradoxically, she denies the validity of the very event (Valis) that created her.
recurring_concepts: **The Two Gods**: A dramatic device in *VALIS* used to depict the internal dynamic process of God's mind, converting the prerational will into rational order (Logos).
reading_excerpt: The "Two Source Cosmogony" in *VALIS* is actually a representation of an internal dialectic *within* the mind of God (as first envisioned by Jakob Böhme), not a battle between God and Satan.
